title: myrobalanus salicifolia
intro: Myrobalanus salicifolia, also known as the Willow Myrobalan, is a plant species within the Anacardiaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Myrobalanus salicifolia include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland)", Myrobalanus salicifolia, which seems to be a misspelling of Myrciaria tenella, has been found to possess antioxidant and antiproliferative activities, as its ethanolic extract (CEE) was shown to promote cell death in tumor cell lines and reduce basal reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels in the Caenorhabditis elegans model.
